# Funding

If you find OpenEtruscan useful for your research, consider supporting its continued development.

## How to Support

### For Researchers
- ⭐ Star the repository — it helps with visibility in the Digital Humanities community
- 📝 Cite OpenEtruscan in your publications
- 📣 Share it with your colleagues and students
- 📊 Contribute your data — every inscription makes the corpus stronger

### For Institutions
- 🏛️ If your department or research group uses OpenEtruscan, consider contributing developer time or student projects
- 📚 Include OpenEtruscan in your digital epigraphy curriculum

### Citation

If you use OpenEtruscan in your research, please cite:

```bibtex
@software{openetruscan,
  title = {OpenEtruscan: Open-Source Tools for Ancient Epigraphy},
  author = {OpenEtruscan Contributors},
  year = {2026},
  version = {0.3.0},
  url = {https://github.com/Eddy1919/openEtruscan},
  license = {MIT}
}
```
